macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed’s Waller says policy is mildly restrictive as inflation remains too high
items.summary: In remarks at a Philadelphia Fed conference, Governor Christopher Waller said monetary policy is appropriately positioned and that the labor market remains stable, while inflation is still above target. He noted that recent tariff and energy-price shocks are pushing prices higher, but expects inflation to gradually return to 2% as shocks fade.

items.title: Markets focused on a busy slate of U.S. data and Fed-related events
items.summary: The latest economic calendar shows investors awaiting and digesting key releases including housing starts, pending home sales, consumer confidence, the Chicago Fed National Activity Index, and speeches from Fed officials. These data points are likely to influence expectations for growth, inflation, and the timing of any future policy moves.

items.title: NGFS releases new report on economic and financial risks from extreme weather
items.summary: The Network for Greening the Financial System published a report examining how extreme weather events can affect macroeconomic conditions and financial stability. The report highlights transmission channels and international implications for policymakers and investors.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Binance sees $924M USDT outflows in 24 hours as crypto traders reposition
items.summary: Binance recorded roughly $924 million in net USDT outflows over a 24-hour period, one of the largest recent stablecoin withdrawals. The move may signal traders shifting to self-custody, decentralized platforms, or adjusting positions amid market stress.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $76.7K as crypto market stays under pressure
items.summary: Bitcoin has been trading around $76,720, roughly flat over the last 24 hours but down about 5.1% on the week. The broader market remains under pressure as traders watch macro conditions and risk appetite.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs reportedly shed nearly $1B in a day amid rising Treasury yields
items.summary: A market update highlighted heavy selling in spot Bitcoin ETFs, with nearly $1 billion in 24-hour outflows and BlackRock’s IBIT among the biggest redemptions. The move came as longer-dated Treasury yields rose sharply, adding pressure to risk assets including crypto.

items.title: SEC preparing innovation exemption for tokenized stocks, signaling push into onchain securities
items.summary: Reports say the SEC is set to unveil an 'innovation exemption' framework that could allow tokenized versions of equities to trade more easily. The proposal would mark a major step toward integrating traditional markets with blockchain-based trading.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass reveals rest-of-May lineup, led by Forza
items.summary: Microsoft has unveiled the Game Pass titles arriving for the remainder of May, with Forza headlining the lineup and available immediately. The announcement adds another major release to the subscription service’s monthly rotation.

items.title: Fortnite returns to the Apple App Store in most regions
items.summary: Fortnite is back on the iPhone App Store in most regions as Epic’s legal battle with Apple continues. The move marks another major development in the long-running dispute over app store policies.

items.title: Nintendo announces Pictonico, a new mobile game based on photos
items.summary: Nintendo has announced Pictonico, a mobile title that turns users’ photos into mini-games. The app is scheduled to launch later this month.

items.title: PlayStation Plus price increases for new members
items.summary: Sony is raising PlayStation Plus pricing for new subscribers, with reported increases of $1/£1 for one-month plans and $3/£3 for three-month plans. Existing members appear unaffected for now.

items.title: GTA 6 release window shifted to November
items.summary: A report notes that Grand Theft Auto 6 was originally planned for spring 2025, but its current release target is November. The change highlights continued anticipation around one of the industry’s biggest upcoming launches.

items.title: Cinematic from cancelled Star Wars KOTOR remake leaks online
items.summary: A cinematic clip from the troubled and reportedly cancelled Knights of the Old Republic remake has surfaced online. The remake was first announced in 2021 and has since faced repeated development issues.

items.title: Virtuos says it wants to bring GTA 5 and Red Dead Redemption 2 to Switch
items.summary: Port specialist Virtuos says it would like to bring Grand Theft Auto 5 and Red Dead Redemption 2 to Nintendo Switch. The idea would be a significant technical challenge given the scope of both games.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Australia's social media ban for minors sparks debate over enforcement and privacy
items.summary: Australia’s new restrictions on minors using social media are drawing scrutiny from governments, platforms, and families over how to reduce online harm without compromising privacy or creating enforcement gaps. The policy is being watched closely as a potential model for other countries.

items.title: Samsung unveils Galaxy Z TriFold with dual-hinge design and 10-inch display
items.summary: Samsung’s latest foldable pushes the category further with a redesigned dual-hinge mechanism, upgraded materials, and a large unfolded display in a pocketable form factor. The device marks one of the company’s most advanced foldable launches to date.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm push Android PCs toward the mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are working to bring Android-based personal computers into broader adoption by extending the mobile operating system to desktop devices. The effort could intensify competition with Microsoft and Apple in the PC market.

items.title: Qualcomm launches Snapdragon W5+ Gen 2 and W5 Gen 2 for wearables
items.summary: Qualcomm’s newest wearable chip platforms focus on improving battery life, connectivity, and industrial design constraints that have limited broader smartwatch adoption. The release is positioned as more than a routine refresh for the wearables market.

items.title: Quantum Motion deploys first full-stack quantum computer made with standard silicon fabrication
items.summary: Quantum Motion says it has built and deployed the first full-stack quantum computer using conventional silicon chip manufacturing processes. If confirmed at scale, the approach could lower barriers to producing more scalable quantum systems.
